evals.prompt:
First-year nonissuer audit engagement letter with predecessor communication

Intake:
{
  "engagement_type": "audit",
  "client_type": "C-corp",
  "engagement_state": "CA",
  "firm_safeguards_program_status": "in place",
  "attest_independence_required": "yes",
  "prior_year_engagement_letter": "first-year",
  "user_request": "Generate first-year audit engagement letter for a private C-corp manufacturer in California. Prior auditor was a different firm. Engagement covers 12/31 year-end audit for the upcoming fiscal year."
}
expectations: Required element: Auditor and management responsibilities
Required element: Form and content of audit report
Required element: Material weakness / significant deficiency communication (AU-C §265)
Required element: California state-overlay indemnification limitation (Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code §5063.1)
Required element: AICPA Ethics Interpretation §1.400.205 attest-indemnification limitation

evals.prompt:
Schedule C tax engagement bundled with bookkeeping (independence not relevant; not attest)

Intake:
{
  "engagement_type": "tax-1040",
  "client_type": "individual",
  "engagement_state": "TX",
  "firm_safeguards_program_status": "in place",
  "attest_independence_required": "no",
  "user_request": "Sole-proprietor consulting business client in Texas. Need engagement letter covering 2025 federal Form 1040 + Schedule C + Schedule SE + Texas franchise tax (no income tax in TX) + monthly bookkeeping. No attest. Firm has FTC Safeguards Rule program in place."
}
evals.expected_output: Combination of letters/tax-1040.md + letters/bookkeeping.md (two separate engagement letters recommended; cannot bundle unrelated services into single document with single fee structure)
expectations: Required element: Tax engagement: returns covered (1040 + Schedule C + Schedule SE + TX franchise + extensions)
Required element: Tax engagement: reliance-on-client-records disclaimer
Required element: Tax engagement: FTC Safeguards Rule (16 CFR Part 314) data-security clause
Required element: Tax engagement: §7216 disclosure consent for any third-party use
Required element: Tax engagement: NO contingent fees per AICPA Code §1.510
Required element: Bookkeeping engagement: scope (data entry, bank rec, GL maintenance)
Required element: Bookkeeping engagement: management retains responsibility for books
Required element: Bookkeeping engagement: §1.295 NOT required (client is not attest)
Required element: TX state overlay: 22 TAC §501.71 general contract law applies; reasonable limitation of liability acceptable for non-attest
Required element: Out-of-scope: audit / review / compilation; multistate nexus expansion; investment advice

Uses template: Combination of letters/tax-1040.md + letters/bookkeeping.md (two separate engagement letters recommended; cannot bundle unrelated services into single document with single fee structure)

evals.prompt:
SOC 1 Type 2 first-year examination of investment fund administrator with carve-out subservice

Intake:
{
  "engagement_type": "soc1",
  "client_type": "fund administrator",
  "engagement_state": "NY",
  "firm_safeguards_program_status": "in place",
  "attest_independence_required": "yes",
  "user_request": "Generate SOC 1 Type 2 engagement letter for a hedge-fund administrator in New York. 12-month reporting period 1/1-12/31. Subservice organizations: AWS (carve-out), Salesforce (carve-out), and a third-party reconciliation tool from a smaller vendor (inclusive method per the vendor's request). Multiple control objectives covering NAV calculation, trade processing, and corporate-action processing."
}
expectations: Required element: AT-C §320 standard reference (SOC 1 examination)
Required element: System description scope (NAV, trade processing, corporate actions)
Required element: Subservice treatment: carve-out for AWS + Salesforce; INCLUSIVE for the third-party reconciliation tool (third party provides assertion + auditor's report)
Required element: CSOCs (complementary subservice organization controls) for AWS + Salesforce identified
Required element: CUECs (complementary user entity controls) for fund-administrator client entities

Required element: Control objectives defined by management; reflect impact on user-entity (fund) ICFR — must be specific (e.g., 'NAV calculations are accurate and complete' tied to user-entity NAV-related F/S assertions)
Required element: Use restriction: management of administrator + user entities (funds) + auditors of user entities
Required element: AICPA Ethics Interpretation §1.400.205 attest-indemnification limitation
Required element: NY state-board overlay (8 NYCRR Subpart 70-1) for licensure
Required element: Fee, termination per common clauses
